original_thesis: The 'think like the attacker' stack. Cybersecurity thinking provides the adversarial mindset; formal reasoning catches logical holes; rationalization-awareness detects self-deception; critical thinking evaluates claims; psychological safety allows uncomfortable truths; systems thinking reveals attack surfaces.
corrected_thesis: The 'detect hidden motives' stack. Rationalization-awareness catches self-deception and motivated reasoning; causal-attribution-resistance prevents premature blame assignment; critical thinking evaluates claims objectively; decomposition isolates which arguments are sound; root-cause-analysis traces to the real driver; scientific method demands evidence over narrative. This stack defeats scenarios where someone (including yourself) is constructing a plausible story instead of finding the truth.
what_this_stack_defeats: Motivated reasoning, rationalization, premature causal attribution, plausible-sounding narratives without evidence
notes: Original thesis described a different member set (cybersecurity-thinking, formal-reasoning, psychological-safety — none are actual members). Shares 5/6 members with Causal Forensics supercluster trunk. Differentiator is rationalization (the adversarial/self-deception angle).
